nativeRange.habitat: Finger Nemastoma is native to shallow marine reef and coastal environments, typically growing in the photic zone (the sunlit upper layer of the water column) attached to hard substrate.

avoidWith: herbivorous fish that may graze macroalgae (e.g., large tangs, rabbitfish)

imageCandidates.notes: Ben Jobson (iNaturalist), 1536x2048. Sharp, vivid in-situ underwater shot of Nemastoma canariense showing the bright red, gelatinous, dichotomously branched finger-like thallus on coralline rock; best genus match. Red macroalgae ID confidence is inherently low.

summary: Finger Nemastoma (Nemastoma sp.) is a gelatinous red macroalgae in the family Nemastomataceae, available in several color forms including orange-tipped, pink, and rose variants. It tends to do well in established reef displays and refugiums but is considered intermediate in care due to its delicate texture and slow holdfast formation.
careNotes:
Finger Nemastoma (Nemastoma sp.) is a specialty red macroalgae in the family Nemastomataceae, order Nemastomatales, class Florideophyceae, phylum Rhodophyta. It is available in four distinct trade color forms: the base red Finger Nemastoma, the Orange-Tipped Nemastoma (which displays fluorescent orange tips visible under actinic lighting), the Pink Thorny Nemastoma, and the Rose Petal Nemastoma. All four color forms share identical husbandry requirements and the same gelatinous, slippery thallus (body tissue) texture. It is a specialty item found at a small number of dedicated macroalgae retailers rather than general reef suppliers.

Anchoring: The thallus is notably thin, gelatinous, and slippery, which makes secure attachment a challenge. Tying it down with fishing line or loosely anchoring it with a clear zip-tie until it develops a holdfast is the generally recommended approach. Holdfast formation tends to be slow and unreliable, so patience and minimal disturbance are advised.

Shipping sensitivity: This species is considered more sensitive to transport stress than many common macroalgae, and overnight or two-day express shipping is typically required for live arrival guarantees.

Reef compatibility: Finger Nemastoma is generally considered safe with standard reef livestock and has been documented serving dual roles as a nutrient-exporting macroalgae and as microhabitat for copepods and amphipods. Compatibility with heavy grazers such as large tangs and rabbitfish should be assessed before adding this species to a reef with established herbivorous fish.

WARNING — DO NOT BURY IN SAND: Like other red macroalgae, Finger Nemastoma will MELT at the base if buried in sand. It should be attached to rock using a zip-tie, fishing line, or by wedging into rockwork. Sand-bed planting is generally suitable only for green macroalgae such as Caulerpa, Halimeda, Penicillus, Rhipocephalus, and Udotea, as well as some calcified members of the Dasycladales.
breedingNotes: Propagation by fragmentation has been reported to be difficult in practice, largely because the gelatinous texture makes cuttings prone to failing to develop a new holdfast. Like other members of the Nemastomataceae, this species undergoes alternation of generations — alternating between a sexual gametophyte phase and an asexual sporophyte phase — but sexual reproduction has not been documented under aquarium conditions.

primary.name: Mosaic Macros
primary.notes: Sole hobby-trade source. Four SKUs cross-verified: Finger Nemastoma, Orange-Tipped Nemastoma, Pink Thorny Nemastoma, Rose Petal Nemastoma. All confirm gelatinous texture, holdfast difficulty, shipping sensitivity, and intermediate care level.

additional.name: Mosaic Macros — Orange-Tipped Nemastoma
additional.notes: Cross-verified: same care notes, fluorescent orange tips under actinic lighting noted as distinguishing visual trait.

additional.name: Mosaic Macros — Pink Thorny Nemastoma
additional.notes: Cross-verified: confirms gelatinous texture, no-glue anchoring requirement, and advanced-beginner care level.

additional.name: Mosaic Macros — Rose Petal Nemastoma
additional.notes: Cross-verified: confirms identical care notes across all four color variants.

additional.name: Mosaic Macros general macroalgae care guide
additional.notes: Slice-wide secondary source — provides authoritative Mosaic-wide parameter targets (temp 22-26°C, pH 8.1-8.4, Ca 400-450 ppm, Mg 1250-1350 ppm, NO3 5-20 ppm, PO4 0.02-0.1 ppm) and the 4-method propagation standard. Added retroactively 2026-06-08.
